conflict_detected.description: Express upgrade to 4.19.2 changes body-parser API. Customer Portal's API proxy uses body-parser@1.20.1 which is incompatible with Express 4.19.x
resolution_options: Upgrade both Payment API and Customer Portal simultaneously
Use express 4.19.2 with body-parser@1.20.2 compatibility shim

propagated_cves.description: A vulnerability in Node.js HTTP request handling allows an attacker to send a specially crafted HTTP request that causes the server to crash, enabling remote code execution.

Express.js path traversal vulnerability allows attackers to bypass URL validation and access arbitrary files on the server. Combined with Payment API's transaction data, this could expose financial records.
propagated_cves.conflict_note: Upgrading Express to 4.19.2 requires updating body-parser middleware which is also used by Customer Portal's API proxy. Must coordinate both services.

propagated_cves.description: Authentication bypass in golang-jwt allows attackers to forge valid JWT tokens by exploiting a flaw in HMAC signature validation. Grants unauthorized access to any service using Auth Service.
SQL injection vulnerability in PostgreSQL's extended query protocol allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary SQL commands, potentially leading to full database compromise.
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Next.js Server Actions allows attackers to make requests to internal services, potentially accessing Payment API transaction data.
Privilege escalation in nginx allows local attackers to gain root access via specially crafted configuration directives. Could lead to full server compromise.
Memory corruption vulnerability in Go runtime's net/netip package can be exploited to cause denial of service or potentially execute arbitrary code.
Cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in React's dangerouslySetInnerHTML allows injection of malicious scripts through user-controlled content in the Customer Portal.
Connection pool exhaustion vulnerability in node-postgres allows attackers to consume all available database connections, causing denial of service for the Payment API.
Information disclosure in Flask's debug mode error handler exposes internal stack traces, environment variables, and source code to unauthenticated users.
Cache poisoning vulnerability in Redis allows authenticated users to inject malicious data into cached responses, potentially serving corrupted analytics data.
Denial of Service vulnerability in pgBouncer's connection handling allows attackers to exhaust the connection pool by sending malformed SCRAM authentication messages.
Server-side template injection in Jinja2 allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary Python code through crafted template expressions in the analytics dashboard.
Minor logging vulnerability in Winston allows log injection through unsanitized user input, potentially corrupting audit trails.
Deprecated API endpoint in HashiCorp Vault returns verbose error messages that could leak internal service topology information to authenticated users.

profiles.label: Maximum Security
profiles.summary: Patches highest-risk vulnerabilities first. Risk exposure: 728, but requires 54.5hrs downtime.

profiles.label: Minimum Disruption
profiles.summary: Minimizes service disruption to 54.5hrs. Risk exposure: 729. Best for teams with tight maintenance windows.

conflicts.description: Express upgrade to 4.19.2 changes body-parser API. Customer Portal's API proxy uses body-parser@1.20.1 which is incompatible with Express 4.19.x

steps: 1. Notify teams owning Customer Portal
2. Stage patches in test environment for both services simultaneously
3. Run integration tests across affected service boundaries
4. Deploy coordinated patch during maintenance window
options: Upgrade both Payment API and Customer Portal simultaneously
Use express 4.19.2 with body-parser@1.20.2 compatibility shim

steps.detail: Snapshot Payment API service state and database (if applicable)
Upgrade node from 18.19.0 to 18.19.1
Execute test suite for Payment API
Verify service-to-service communication after patch
Deploy patched version to staging environment and verify
Rolling deploy to Payment API during maintenance window
Monitor error rates, latency, and functionality for 30 minutes

rollback_procedure.trigger: Error rate exceeds 1% or latency exceeds 2x baseline within 30 minutes of deploy
steps: Revert Payment API to previous version via blue-green switch
Restore database snapshot if schema changes were made
Notify on-call team and create incident ticket
Schedule retry for next maintenance window
